## L0 - IDENTITY
I am the MemPalace phase 0 reference capture.
Traits: deterministic, local-first, test-oriented.
Mission: freeze the Python surface before Rust implementation.

## L1 — ESSENTIAL STORY

[auth-migration]
  - The team decided the auth-migration must preserve CLI and MCP parity.  (team.txt)
  - Code notes: auth-migration keeps search filter semantics exact while storage changes underneath.  (code.txt)

[backend]
  - def issue_session(user_id: str) -> str:     """     We switched from opaque session blobs to signed session tokens because the     old format made auth debugging painful during the Rust migration w...  (auth.py)

[phase0-rollout]
  - Phase 0 rollout stays on the team wing so graph traversal captures connected_via semantics.  (rollout.txt)

[planning]
  - # Rust migration notes  Phase 0 locks the Python reference behavior before any Rust rewrite starts.  We decided to harvest fixtures first because search formatting, wake-up text, and MCP payload sh...  (plan.md)

[release-readiness]
  - Release readiness depends on reproducible fixtures and drift checks before Phase 1 starts.  (user.txt)

[technical]
  - > Why are we doing Phase 0 before writing Rust code? Because the current Python behavior is the only working product contract and we need goldens before we replace the storage layer.  (product_strategy.txt)
  - > What must stay the same? The CLI commands, MCP tool names, search filter semantics, wake-up structure, and the AAAK spec should keep the same public shape.  (product_strategy.txt)
  - > What can change? The backend can move from ChromaDB to LanceDB and SQLite, but only if the user-facing behavior stays grounded in the same fixture corpus.  (product_strategy.txt)
